channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Who should regulate global AI? China makes a play for leadership
item.description: The AI world is moving at lightning speed – and the world is trying to figure out how to regulate this technology. China just made a big play to be the global leader.
item.content:encoded: The AI world is moving at lightning speed – and the world is trying to figure out how to regulate this technology. China just made a big play to be the global leader.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Engineers develop a bird-scale flapping robot for aerial-aquatic travel
item.description: Researchers have developed a new kind of robot that flies through the air, swims through the water, and transitions between the two realms.
item.content:encoded: Researchers have developed a new kind of robot that flies through the air, swims through the water, and transitions between the two realms.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: ICE shared Medicaid data it wasn't supposed to have with Palantir
item.description: The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.
item.content:encoded:
The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.

(Image credit: Adam Gray)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Kalshi says it's not a sportsbook even as World Cup bets surge
item.description: The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.
item.content:encoded:
The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.

(Image credit: Erin Hooley/AP Photo/Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
item.description: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.
item.content:encoded:
American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Officials probe whether White House teleprompter operator profited off Trump's words
item.description: It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.
item.content:encoded:
It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: American AI is expensive. Some startups are turning to cheap Chinese models
item.description: AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.
item.content:encoded:
AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.

(Image credit: Imen Ben Youssef/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: How do young people feel about AI? 7 teens weigh in
item.description: What's it like to grow up and learn in the age of AI? NPR put that question to seven teenagers across the country.
item.content:encoded: What's it like to grow up and learn in the age of AI? NPR put that question to seven teenagers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: 'The Trojan Teddy Bear': The promise and peril of childhood in the age of AI
item.description: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.content:encoded: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Political text messages could get more effective and annoying. Blame AI
item.description: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.
item.content:encoded:
Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid
